title: If you could write a PhD thesis
display_title: If you could write a PhD thesis
wikitext:
[]: If you could write a Ph.D. thesis...             Quote Post by C.W.Robson46290 » Tue Feb 14, 2006 10:43 pm Alright. My favorite part of the whole Evangelion experience is the philosophy. On that note, what is your favorite let's call them concepts, no, revelations if you will, that NGE instilled in your mind? It could be something in particular that made you think differently, made you scratch your head...whatever, I'm lenient. Personally I am a huge fan of the idea that you can only create and identify yourself truly through the similarities and differences you have with other people. P.S.: If this is a ridiculously redone string, let me know please. I don't want to re-hash a discussion that doesn't need to be rehashed. If this is overdone I apologize in advance!

[]:             Quote Post by tokenflipguy » Wed Feb 15, 2006 1:17 am I am doing one based around the psychological makeup of three characters: Shinji, Asuka, and Misato. I am barely in the drafting stages.

[]:             Quote Post by poetic_assassin » Wed Feb 15, 2006 3:13 pm I like the A.T. feild theory. Here's a real conception of it. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Morphogenetic_field

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Feb 15, 2006 6:27 pm tokenflipguy wrote: I am doing one based around the psychological makeup of three characters: Shinji, Asuka, and Misato. I am barely in the drafting stages.

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Thu Feb 16, 2006 5:35 am in a few years time this'll be me when the time comes i'll be able to alienate verybody i know all over again. i'd have to go with the psychological side of things aswell, it's just what makes evangelion, evangelion. however, i'd choose the relationship between Shinji and gendo and possibly Misato and Kaji, though there really isn't much to say about the latter.

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Mon Feb 20, 2006 7:10 am On that note, what is your favorite let's call them concepts, no, revelations if you will, that NGE instilled in your mind? It could be something in particular that made you think differently, made you scratch your head...whatever, I'm lenient.

[]:             Quote Post by C.W.Robson46290 » Sat Jul 01, 2006 8:49 pm I really wasn't trying to limit the discussion in anyway other than ideas asociated with the philosophcial/psychological aspects taht are so obviously prominent in NGE. My question could have been something that made you think differently, something taht helped to shape your own ideas, or something taht seemed so blatantly obvious to you that you now concretely beliee just that. Anything of that nature would be perfectly within the intention I had for this thread. Thank you for trying to clarify before posting.

[]:             Quote Post by Ripple_in_Eternity » Sat Jul 08, 2006 12:06 am [deleted by user]

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Jul 08, 2006 12:32 am The Miato Shinji Asuka relation ship their so alike.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sat Jul 08, 2006 5:31 am I liked the AT-Field theory too. The whole story is written in my soul with flaming runes for eternity. The most memorable scene was in EoE, the last part. I was like O_O for the rest of the day. After half an hour, all I could say was "Oh my God... Merciful Tempus... what the fuck..." and such things. Having limits is a wonderful thing, Ripple. No, seriously. For example, when the teachers make me angry, I thank God that I don't have anything with which I could tell them how I feel about them (flamethrower, hand grenade, two hungry bloodhounds and/or hellhounds, magic in general, and so on...).

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sat Jul 08, 2006 7:47 am AT Field theory, as well. The Hedgehog complex seems too true, and NGE basically gave humans the hedgehog spikes. AT Fields cause sorrows, cause pain, make people different. They spawn hate, lies, cotempt, and angst in general. What I didn't like about NGE is that only dealt with the pain. Individualty does have it's good points. What of companionship, and love between family and one's beloved? It would appear that Shinji and Yui's love was what, eventually, led Shinji to Third Impact and it's rejection. Maybe she was what led Shinji to emerge from the Sea of LCL. Ugh, NGE is so ambigous... And that's why I love it.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sat Jul 08, 2006 1:35 pm AT Fields cause sorrows, cause pain, make people different.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sat Jul 08, 2006 1:48 pm Kaworu says AT Fields are the thread that binds the soul. An AT Field crumbles, a person's persona shatters. So the AT Field holds the persona. Now, look at the Hedgehog Dillema- The closer the hedgehogs come together, the more their spikes hurt one another. Anno just took people and gave them the spikes. Those spikes, are, in fact, AT Fields. Now, Anno goes into the assumption that everyone have every mental illness and personality disorder to SOME extent. That is true for the most part. Everyone get miserable because people don't understand them, it's just some people can hide and deal with it. It doesn't make the problem go away, nor does the pain vanish- People STILL don't understand one another. That's the whole point- Since people do not understand one another, that creates anger. Anger turns to rage, and that in turn spawns hate. Hate spawns more and more negative emotions, I won't go Yoda here... You know where this'll go. Those negative emotions make people hurt. They come closer, argue, get angry and eventually break each other's hearts. Shinji's heart was broken time after time in Evangelion, which makes him the prime victim of Hedgehog's Dillema. That makes it all the more ironic that he was the one who rejected what could be called the cure to that problem. Now, if people become a collective consiousness one could say they understand each other... Even though they don't. They understand each other the same way someone understands himself. They share the same emotions, ideas, thoughts... A true collective consiousness. Rei says it's a world without pain and sorrow, but at the same time it's a world without love and hope. Evangelion is much the same-it neglects the good emotions and focuses on pain, on horror, on insanity. In that regard one could say 'sad-ending stories are seldom popular', but there's nothing bad with sad endings... It's bad when there is truly no hope shown at the story. NGE never really delved into Shinji really falling in love, when there were oh so many chances with Asuka, Rei, even Misato! Why? Why not touch on love? Isn't that a positive emotion? Doesn't that trigger hope?

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sat Jul 08, 2006 2:36 pm I guess our point is that since humanity gained the "free will" ability, we also picked up the "asshole" feat, and this has to be fixed. Whatever we say, our crap is our responsibility. It is this simple. Pain shocks you. Shock forces you to think things over. It is this simple. And not everybody is such a happy guy like you (But please, don't get angry. I didn't mean to mock you. In truth, I'm damn jealous of happy people). A lot of people are sad and depressed, and they tend to have a good reason for it, but there are simple weaklings too. Being sad and depressed is... convenient. Shinji is a great example. But fighting for a better life... it is hard. For this you can't really find examples. All who have tried have fallen thanks to their ignorance. Misato, Gendo, Ritsuko, Prof. Katsuragi... mostly adults, as I see. The kids were but tools in there hands, nothing more. Though even a tool could be troublesome (Shinji), too "toolish" (Ayanami) or too impulsive (Asuka). I found that if I put "aura" in the place of "AT-Field", it makes sense. Don't you think? And AT-Field is not a spike. The spike is the visible crap of one's personality. Is it so hard to imagine? And what is said in Evangelion is not the Bible. The key word in Evangelion is responsibility. English is a good language: "the ability to respond". It is like this in other languages too. Who has this virtue in the story? In some cases many character has, but all of them lack another virtue called "persistance". And I don't think that becoming one with my grandmother would solve any problems. She would never accept/understand that role playing games are not satanic. Becoming one would mean an endless and hopeless age of conflict. It'd be Hell I guess. No, I'm sure. Absolutely sure. Becoming one is not equal with co-existence. Because what we can see in EoE, is a great mass of totally confused ignorant human souls, and the whole scene is pretty chaotic. Not that chaos is a bad thing, but in that case it is well beyond the level of healthy. Oh, and believe me: death is a good thing too. It is another way to peace. Love is the other way. Life is not black and white; it is colourful. It has more colours than your computer can imagine.

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sat Jul 08, 2006 2:56 pm I am not offended and am quite aware not everyone's happy. Heck, my gf's depressed. That's not the point, however. A 'spike' is a metaphor. You can say that the closer people come together, their AT Fields knock them away from each other. Put Hedgehogs instead of people and spikes instead of AT Fields, and it still makes sense. Anno did something rather intresting, he decided to address the Hedgehog Dillema head on in this way. As for people being depressed-I'm not saying those people are wrong. I like tho think of myself as a future psychologist, and realize that your mental condition is just as natural as mine. But I'm not calling depression a positive emotion. That's the point- Anno address a problem, but not it's solution. It's like asking a question and not expecting an answer. And as far as merging with your grandma... Well, merging with your grandma would either make her like video games, you dislike video games, or reach some kind of happy medium. The nature of the Human Instrumental Collective-Consiousness is very unexplored, and the only thing we know about it is that you can't tell where you begin and other people end. That's about that. Oh, and let's not forget people won't conflict. What you gotta realize is that you lose yourself in the Sea of LCL. There is no longer a 'me', there is only an 'us' and that becomes a bigger 'me'. That bigger 'me' is not you, but more like everyone thrown together into a blender and spinned for so long their blood turned orange.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sun Jul 09, 2006 12:29 pm Just why do you think that AT-Fields are so bad? And what do you say for the "AT-Field=aura" theory? You didn't say a word about that. You are saying your version and I'm barking mine in this case. So what about this...?

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Jul 09, 2006 12:43 pm I've never said AT Fields are bad. I just say they are the source of human sorrow. I look at human sorrow as neither good or bad, but rather part of man's existence. A part which can't be seperated. And I'm not certain what you mean by Aura. An aura can be a sort of circle above an angel's head or a little blue flame which the undead feed off, or some kind of light that surrounds you and keeps you sane. Define Aura, then I'll talk about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sun Jul 09, 2006 1:16 pm When I say "aura", I'm thinking about an electro-magnetic field generated by the body and the soul, in which the observed one's emotions, thoughts and memories (both the current and those gathered from the past lives) are stored. The aura of the soul is much like the limbs of the body. For example telekinesis (as I think of it) is when the soul reaches out for something, and "thickens" itself to be able to manipulate in the material world (since the aura is based in the astral plane (it is the co-existence of the planes of thoughts and emotions), though it effects the material world). Those who can see the aura can only observe the top of the ocean, though the better the eye the clearer we see things around us, don't you think? This what I mean when I say "aura".

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Jul 09, 2006 1:28 pm In real life I somehwhat doubt the possiblity of an Aura, but even then I've heard some intresting things about negative and positive energy, so I won't rule you out here in regards to this. However, it does seem to fit mostly into the criteria of an AT Field. An AT Field strikes me as something more, though. I mean, Kaworu calls it a 'thread'. It sort of forms a symbiotic relationship between the soul and the aura: The Aura protects the soul from going into Instrumentality mode and the Soul gives the Aura the power needed to protect it. Kind of like the Nuclei-Mitochondria relationship: The Nucleus provides oxygen, the Mito provides energy.

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Sun Jul 09, 2006 1:34 pm I thought the fact that you can't have one thing without the other particulary interesting. The whole AT field concept and the hedgehogs dillema were also interesting ideas
summary: []: If you could write a Ph.D. thesis...             Quote Post by C.W.Robson46290 » Tue Feb 14, 2006 10:43 pm Alright. My favorite part of the whole Evangelion experience is the philosophy. On that note, what is your favorite let's call them concepts, no, revelations if you will, that NGE instilled in your mind? It could be something in particular that made you think differently, made you scratch your head...whatever, I'm lenient.
